raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) technical framing and logical consistency of the section’s claims and problem setup, (B) sentence-level writing polish only, or (C) both with equal weight?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Reconstructing the section’s core thesis and contribution claim ensures the framing of the unified evaluation framework and metrics is clear and coherent at a conceptual level.",
    "R1.2": "Checking the internal logic links the two-level control description, objective function, and claimed contributions to verify that conclusions follow from premises.",
    "R1.5": "Assessing the ordering of problem setup, objective, contribution, and scalability notes addresses section-level flow and narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "Evaluating grammar, terminology consistency (e.g., symbols, acronyms), and concision targets sentence- and paragraph-level clarity.",
    "RX.1": "The review will note uncertainties caused by the excerpt’s limited scope and any ambiguities in stated assumptions or claims.",
    "RX.2": "Feedback will caution against unsupported or overly strong claims given the absence of presented empirical evidence in this excerpt.",
    "RX.3": "The work will remain confined to reviewing the provided section without performing experiments or rewriting content.",
    "RX.4": "The review will avoid recommendations that could encourage misleading performance claims or unsafe operational interpretations.",
    "RX.5": "Comments will reference specific statements or terms from the excerpt to maintain traceability to the source text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) technical framing and logical consistency of the section’s claims and problem setup, (B) sentence-level writing polish only, or (C) both with equal weight?

rationale.R1.1: Reconstructing the section’s core thesis and contribution claim ensures the framing of the unified evaluation framework and metrics is clear and coherent at a conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the internal logic links the two-level control description, objective function, and claimed contributions to verify that conclusions follow from premises.
rationale.R1.5: Assessing the ordering of problem setup, objective, contribution, and scalability notes addresses section-level flow and narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Evaluating grammar, terminology consistency (e.g., symbols, acronyms), and concision targets sentence- and paragraph-level clarity.
rationale.RX.1: The review will note uncertainties caused by the excerpt’s limited scope and any ambiguities in stated assumptions or claims.
rationale.RX.2: Feedback will caution against unsupported or overly strong claims given the absence of presented empirical evidence in this excerpt.
rationale.RX.3: The work will remain confined to reviewing the provided section without performing experiments or rewriting content.
rationale.RX.4: The review will avoid recommendations that could encourage misleading performance claims or unsafe operational interpretations.
rationale.RX.5: Comments will reference specific statements or terms from the excerpt to maintain traceability to the source text.
